security_gap.gap_description: High volume of swap transactions can impact BEX availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks are possible, mitigated by gas fees and network capacity.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient $BERA to pay gas for many transactions.
Berachain network has limited transaction throughput or gas fees are low enough to make flooding economically feasible.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure sufficient network capacity and appropriate transaction fee mechanisms.

description: An attacker could flood the BEX contract with swap requests, potentially impacting its availability for other users.

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of liquidity deposits can impact BEX availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, mitigated by gas fees and network capacity.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient $BERA and deposit assets.
Network capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure sufficient network capacity and appropriate transaction fee mechanisms.

description: An attacker could flood the BEX contract with liquidity deposit transactions, potentially impacting the ability of legitimate users to provide liquidity.

origin.capability_match: Stake LP Token in Reward Vault

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of staking transactions can impact Reward Vault availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, mitigated by gas fees and network capacity.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient $BERA and LP tokens.
Network capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure sufficient network capacity and appropriate transaction fee mechanisms.

description: An attacker could flood a Reward Vault contract with staking transactions, potentially impacting the ability of legitimate users to stake their LP tokens.

trace_source.matched_capability: Stake LP Token in Reward Vault

security_gap.gap_description: The claim logic might be vulnerable, allowing users to claim excessive $BGT.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Integrity flaws in claim logic could lead to unauthorized BGT minting/claiming.

assumptions: The Reward Vault contract or its BGT distribution logic contains a bug.
The attacker can craft a malicious transaction to exploit the bug.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust smart contract auditing and testing.

description: A vulnerability in the $BGT claim logic within the Reward Vault could allow an attacker to claim more $BGT than they are legitimately entitled to.

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of claim transactions can impact Reward Vault availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, mitigated by gas fees and network capacity.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient $BERA.
Network capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure sufficient network capacity and appropriate transaction fee mechanisms.

description: An attacker could flood a Reward Vault contract with claim transactions, potentially impacting the ability of legitimate users to claim their $BGT rewards.

security_gap.gap_description: Delegation logic might be vulnerable, allowing manipulation of validator boost.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Integrity flaws in delegation logic could disrupt the PoL mechanism.

assumptions: The delegation contract/logic contains a bug.
The attacker can craft a malicious transaction to exploit the bug.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust smart contract auditing and testing for delegation logic.

description: A vulnerability in the $BGT delegation logic could allow an attacker to manipulate the amount of $BGT delegated to a validator, unfairly impacting their boost and emissions.

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of delegation transactions can impact delegation system availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, mitigated by gas fees and network capacity.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient $BERA.
Network capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure sufficient network capacity and appropriate transaction fee mechanisms.

description: An attacker could flood the delegation system with transactions, potentially impacting the ability of legitimate users to delegate their $BGT.

security_gap.gap_description: Voting mechanism might not sufficiently verify the voter's identity or prevent vote tampering.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromised voting integrity could subvert governance decisions.

assumptions: The governance contract or voting mechanism lacks strong authentication/signature verification.
An attacker can intercept or craft vote transactions.
Voting power calculation is accurate and tied to BGT.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust authentication and signature verification for voting.

description: An attacker could submit fraudulent votes by impersonating a legitimate $BGT holder or tampering with vote transactions, potentially influencing governance outcomes.

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of vote transactions can impact Governance system availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, mitigated by gas fees and network capacity.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient $BERA.
Network capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure sufficient network capacity and appropriate transaction fee mechanisms.

description: An attacker could flood the Governance contract with transactions, potentially impacting the ability of legitimate users to cast their votes.

security_gap.gap_description: The proof generation or claim logic might be vulnerable, allowing users to claim excessive incentives.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Integrity flaws could lead to unauthorized incentive claiming and economic loss for other users/protocols.

assumptions: The backend API or proof generation mechanism has a vulnerability.
The BGTIncentiveDistributor contract contains a bug in claim validation.
The attacker can craft a malicious proof or transaction.

mitigation.strategy: Secure the backend API generating proofs and ensure robust smart contract validation of proofs.

description: A vulnerability in the incentive claim proof generation or validation logic could allow an attacker to claim more Incentive Tokens than they are legitimately entitled to.

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of claim transactions can impact Distributor contract availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, mitigated by gas fees and network capacity.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient $BERA.
Network capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure sufficient network capacity and appropriate transaction fee mechanisms.

description: An attacker could flood the BGTIncentiveDistributor contract with claim transactions, potentially impacting the ability of legitimate users to claim their Incentive Tokens.

security_gap.gap_description: The minting logic might be vulnerable, allowing users to mint $HONEY without sufficient collateral.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Integrity flaws in minting could depeg $HONEY and cause economic instability.

assumptions: The HoneyFactory or related vault contracts contain a bug in collateral validation or mint amount calculation.
The attacker can craft a malicious transaction.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust smart contract auditing and testing for minting logic and collateral handling.

description: A vulnerability in the $HONEY minting logic could allow an attacker to mint $HONEY without depositing required collateral or to mint excessive amounts, depegging the stablecoin.

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of mint transactions can impact HoneyFactory availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, mitigated by gas fees and network capacity.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient $BERA.
Network capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure sufficient network capacity and appropriate transaction fee mechanisms.

description: An attacker could flood the HoneyFactory contract with mint transactions, potentially impacting the ability of legitimate users to mint $HONEY.

security_gap.gap_description: The burning/redemption logic might be vulnerable, allowing users to redeem excessive collateral.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Integrity flaws in burning/redemption could drain collateral pools and depeg $HONEY.

assumptions: The HoneyFactory or related vault contracts contain a bug in burn validation or redemption amount calculation.
The attacker can craft a malicious transaction.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust smart contract auditing and testing for burning/redemption logic and collateral handling.

description: A vulnerability in the $HONEY burning/redemption logic could allow an attacker to redeem more collateral than they are legitimately entitled to by burning $HONEY, potentially draining collateral pools.

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of burn transactions can impact HoneyFactory availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, mitigated by gas fees and network capacity.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient $BERA.
Network capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure sufficient network capacity and appropriate transaction fee mechanisms.

description: An attacker could flood the HoneyFactory contract with burn transactions, potentially impacting the ability of legitimate users to burn $HONEY and redeem collateral.

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of sufficient validation or authentication could allow fraudulent bridge transfers.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Vulnerabilities in the bridge could lead to unauthorized asset minting or loss.

assumptions: The bridge protocol (LayerZero) or its implementation contains vulnerabilities.
Weak validation of external chain events or signatures.

mitigation.strategy: Perform rigorous security audits of the bridge protocol and smart contracts.

description: An attacker could exploit vulnerabilities in the bridging process to mint unauthorized assets on Berachain or steal assets from the source chain by submitting fraudulent bridge claims.

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of bridge transactions can impact bridge service availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, mitigated by gas fees on both chains and bridge infrastructure.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient funds on either chain to pay fees.
The bridge infrastructure has capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure bridge infrastructure is robust and handle high load.

description: An attacker could flood the bridge service with transactions, preventing legitimate users from transferring assets between networks.

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of sufficient validation or authentication could allow fraudulent NFT bridge transfers.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Vulnerabilities in the NFT bridge could lead to unauthorized NFT minting or loss.

assumptions: The bridge protocol (LayerZero) or its implementation contains vulnerabilities.
Weak validation of external chain events or signatures for NFT ownership.

mitigation.strategy: Perform rigorous security audits of the NFT bridge protocol and smart contracts.

description: An attacker could exploit vulnerabilities in the NFT bridging process to mint unauthorized NFTs on Berachain or steal NFTs from the source chain by submitting fraudulent bridge claims.

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of bridge transactions can impact bridge service availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, mitigated by gas fees on both chains and bridge infrastructure.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient funds on either chain to pay fees.
The bridge infrastructure has capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure bridge infrastructure is robust and handle high load.

description: An attacker could flood the NFT bridge service with transactions, preventing legitimate users from transferring their NFTs between networks.

security_gap.gap_description: Allocation data stored by the checker service might be accessible without proper authorization.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Disclosure of sensitive allocation data could lead to targeted attacks or privacy loss.

assumptions: The Airdrop Checker Service has vulnerabilities allowing unauthorized data access.
User allocation data is stored in a queryable format.

mitigation.strategy: Secure the Airdrop Checker Service database and API with strong authentication and access controls.

description: An attacker could exploit vulnerabilities in the Airdrop Checker Service to gain unauthorized access to user $BERA allocation data.

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of check requests can impact Checker Service availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, depends on service infrastructure capacity.

assumptions: Attacker can send a high volume of requests.
The Checker Service infrastructure has capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure service infrastructure is robust and handle high load.

description: An attacker could flood the Airdrop Checker Service with requests, preventing legitimate users from checking their allocation.

security_gap.gap_description: The claim logic might be vulnerable, allowing users to claim excessive or unauthorized $BERA.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Integrity flaws in claim logic could lead to unauthorized $BERA distribution.

assumptions: The Airdrop Claim Contract contains a bug in allocation validation or claim logic.
The attacker can craft a malicious transaction to exploit the bug.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust smart contract auditing and testing for airdrop claim logic.

description: A vulnerability in the Airdrop Claim Contract could allow an attacker to claim more $BERA than their allocated amount or claim the airdrop for another user.

security_gap.gap_description: High volume of claim transactions can impact Claim Contract availability.

actor_risk.description: Represents any user interacting with the Berachain dApps and contracts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: High volume attacks possible, mitigated by gas fees and network capacity.

assumptions: Attacker has sufficient $BERA.
Network capacity limitations.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure sufficient network capacity and appropriate transaction fee mechanisms.

description: An attacker could flood the Airdrop Claim Contract with transactions, potentially preventing legitimate users from claiming their $BERA airdrop.
